mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate eases discounting pressure and lifts equity valuations, so lower FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if lower FEDFUNDS does not precede higher 20d SPX returns in the out-of-sample sample, or if the sign of the effect flips during easing periods for more than half of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment above a short-term threshold signals a worsening labor market and weaker future earnings, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment spikes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after triggers is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and industrial production both improve together, the market is observing broad demand momentum that supports near-term earnings surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rise in RSAFS and INDPRO does not outperform either series alone in explaining 20d SPX returns, or if the combined signal has no positive conditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production indicates stronger output and capacity utilization, which should translate into positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO gains are not followed by positive average 20d SPX returns, or if the signal becomes negative in most rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound above a depressed level signals stronger rate-sensitive demand and improving household balance-sheet confidence, which should support S&P 500 gains over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts rebounds fail to precede positive 60d SPX returns, or if the average return after the trigger is not above the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payrolls slowdown signals weaker income growth and lower future consumption, so declining PAYEMS should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if payroll declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the average forward return after the trigger is non-negative.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high policy rates and elevated inflation creates a discount-rate and margin-compression shock, so FEDFUNDS above neutral while CPIAUCSL is still rising should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is rising on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation state does not produce negative 20d SPX returns on average, or if the sign is not worse than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDP and employment indicates broad cyclical acceleration that should lift equity earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and payroll acceleration fails to predict positive 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant across successive evaluation windows.
